# openshift_compatibility (Data Source)

Returns the provider's OCP compatibility matrix.



## Example Usage

### Guard against unsupported OCP versions at plan time

Read the compatibility matrix and use a `precondition` to fail fast if the OCP version you intend to deploy is flagged as broken in the current provider release. This prevents a long cluster install from starting only to fail mid-way due to a known provider incompatibility.

```hcl
data "openshift_compatibility" "current" {}

locals {
  ocp_version = "4.16"

  # Find the matrix entry for the chosen version (null if not listed).
  compat_entry = one([
    for entry in data.openshift_compatibility.current.matrix :
    entry if entry.ocp_version == local.ocp_version
  ])
}

resource "openshift_cluster" "prod" {
  cluster_name         = "prod"
  base_dns_domain      = "example.internal"
  openshift_version    = local.ocp_version
  pull_secret          = var.pull_secret
  ssh_public_key       = var.ssh_public_key

  api_vip              = "192.168.10.10"
  ingress_vip          = "192.168.10.11"
  machine_network_cidr = "192.168.10.0/24"

  create_infra_env = true
  auto_install     = false

  lifecycle {
    precondition {
      condition = (
        local.compat_entry == null ||
        !local.compat_entry.broken
      )
      error_message = "OCP ${local.ocp_version} is marked broken by this provider version: ${try(local.compat_entry.broken_reason, "see compatibility matrix")}."
    }
  }
}

# Optionally output the full matrix for inspection.
output "compatibility_matrix" {
  value = data.openshift_compatibility.current.matrix
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `matrix` (Attributes List) List of OCP version compatibility entries. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--matrix))

<a id="nestedatt--matrix"></a>
### Nested Schema for `matrix`

Read-Only:

- `broken` (Boolean)
- `broken_reason` (String)
- `k8s_api_versions` (List of String)
- `min_oc_mirror` (String)
- `min_provider_ver` (String)
- `ocp_version` (String)
